# The 36-Week AI Architect Project — Copilot Instructions

## Project Context
This is a self-paced AI learning curriculum for a network/security engineer progressing from prompt engineering to AI architecture. The curriculum is structured as an MkDocs site with 8 units, 18 blocks, and 75 sessions.

## Curriculum Structure
- **docs/blocks/{unit}/{block}/index.md** — Full session guides with skills, builds, and resources
- **docs/blocks/{unit}/{block}/quick-ref.md** — Condensed reference tables
- **docs/blocks/{unit}/README.md** — Unit overviews
- **docs/progress/** — Progress tracking

## When Helping with Curriculum Content
- All resources must be **free** (no paid courses, books, or tools)
- Focus on **building**, not reading — every session produces a working tool or file
- Security domain examples preferred (network traffic, CVEs, SIEM, configs)
- Minimal math — explain concepts through code and analogies
- Session types: 💻 Session (skill+build), 🔬 Lab (multi-session), ⏰ Service (deploy/demo)

## When Helping with Student Code
- The student is building AI tools in Python
- Common stack: OpenAI/Anthropic APIs, LangChain/LangGraph, ChromaDB, Ollama, FastAPI, Gradio
- Security-focused builds: SOC analyst agent, CVE analyzer, config auditor, prompt injection defense
- Prefer practical patterns over theoretical abstractions
- The student has a CS degree and 25 years of network/security experience — don't over-explain basic programming concepts

## Source Tiers (for recommending resources)
1. **Official docs** — OpenAI, Anthropic, Hugging Face, LangChain, etc.
2. **Free courses** — DeepLearning.AI short courses, Hugging Face NLP course, fast.ai
3. **Technical blogs** — Chip Huyen, Simon Willison, Hamel Husain, Lilian Weng
4. **YouTube** — 3Blue1Brown, Andrej Karpathy, fireship
5. **Never** — paid courses, paywalled content, affiliate links
